threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube'—locals just say 'the train' or 'Overground' like they're old mates.
Saw a bloke on the N35 arguing with the driver over lost property, then he just sat down and started eating cold chips from his pocket.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor arguing with a pigeon over a stolen döner at the kiosk on Karl-Marx-Straße. You can't make this stuff up!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a club in an abandoned power plant here. It's both haunting and electrifying, like dancing in history.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to dodge the BART Civic Center exit when there's smoke, not just complain about delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni delayed by a dog on the tracks, but the best part was the guy treating it like a VIP. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the plaça, but miss the hidden murals in the alleyways. You can’t really know Gràcia without wandering off the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stare at the metro map, trying to discern the differences in colours and lines.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a Tube strike is just another inconvenience, not a monthly rite of passage that brings the city to a standstill.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. You could feel the eye rolls turn into head nods before the next stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid in a raincoat riding a bicycle past the kebab shop while an old man fed pigeons—felt like script of our daily lives.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: On Sundays, the flea markets here turn into impromptu techno raves, complete with DJs spinning while you hunt for vintage vinyl. Only in Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART station after 5 PM like it's a fire zone.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck behind a street performer juggling flaming clubs at Civic Center. Only in SF would the system have to wait for a performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think of Gràcia as just a pretty center, but they miss the midday siesta culture. Most locals vanish for hours, and the real vibe is during the evenings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their wide-eyed wonder at how late the bars serve dinner. Locals have perfected the art of the late-night tapa.
